disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began 5 days ago as a viral coryzal illness (mild congestion, clear rhinorrhoea) and have been present under 10 days
trigger_question_examples: How did it begin — was it a cold at first?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It kicked off about five days ago just like a normal cold — stuffy nose, clear runny nose — and then really flared up in the last two days.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Facial pain and pressure over the cheeks and forehead, markedly worse on bending forward, with a purulent nasal discharge that turned thick and discoloured over the last 48 hours
trigger_question_examples: Does anything make the pressure worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's right across my cheeks and forehead and it gets dramatically worse the second I lean forward. The stuff coming out of my nose has gone thick and yellow-green.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Associated upper-tooth ache, reduced sense of smell, and a post-nasal-drip cough worse at night
trigger_question_examples: Any other symptoms — teeth, smell, cough?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now you mention it, my top teeth ache, I can barely smell anything, and I've got an annoying cough at night from drip down the back of my throat.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No visual disturbance, no eye swelling or redness, no pain on eye movement (orbital red-flag screen negative on patient report)
trigger_question_examples: Any swelling or redness around your eyes?
Any double vision, blurred vision or pain when you move your eyes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my eyes look normal — no puffiness or redness, no double vision, and it doesn't hurt to move them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No severe unremitting headache, no neck stiffness, no photophobia and no confusion (intracranial red-flag screen negative on patient report)
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a severe headache, a stiff neck, or discomfort with light?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No stiff neck, bright light is fine, and I'm thinking clearly — it's really the facial pressure that's the problem.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms have been steadily worsening for 5 days without an initial improvement then relapse (no biphasic 'double sickening' pattern)
trigger_question_examples: Did you start to get better at any point and then get worse again?
Has it been getting steadily worse or did it come in two waves?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it never really improved — it's just been getting steadily worse the whole time, not better-then-worse.
